note: Phase G — the agent/subagent contract spine. SCOPE: these are .mjs TOOLING deliverables, not app TS symbols. The cert/gate/status loop is TypeScript-symbol-scoped (recon: extract.mjs runs ts-morph allowJs:false), so these changes are STRUCTURE-ONLY here and are NOT verified by flowmap:gate. Their real verification is their own node --test suites + flowmap:replay (byte-determinism). This plan exists to make the build's own structure a coherence-checked artifact (C3), nothing more.

intent.problem: byte-identical output across 100 runs needs ONE canonicaliser+hasher; per-tool JSON.stringify does not sort keys (recon).
intent.approach: tools/flowmap/lib/canonical.mjs: canonicalize (recursive key sort, arrays keep order), canonicalJSON, sha256hex, hashOf.
intent.rationale: single source of truth for determinism; everything downstream depends on it.

intent.problem: a 0-context subagent needs a self-contained, deterministic packet per change, not prose.
intent.approach: flowmap:contract --change <id> --json routes to parseMmd + srcDirectives + the REAL downstreamCone + checkPlan; emits a canonical packet with a content hash.
intent.rationale: route, not compute — reuse existing computation so the packet cannot drift from the map.

intent.problem: the result that escapes a subagent must be a tool-computed verdict, not a model-judged narration.
intent.approach: flowmap:verify-change --change <id> --json routes to status.mjs --json + runAcceptance; folds into a data-only PASS/FAIL verdict with a content hash.
intent.rationale: the agent proposes, a tool disposes; 100 correct solutions return 100 identical verdicts.

intent.problem: schema-lock fixes shape, not determinism; a judgement leak hides inside a well-shaped field.
intent.approach: flowmap:replay --task <cmd> --n N runs the task N times and asserts one stdout hash + one exit status; fails on divergence.
intent.rationale: the BINDING acceptance test — it is the only thing that proves the verdict routes instead of computes.

intent.problem: 'subagents go through the contract' is only a convention unless something enforces it at spawn time.
intent.approach: PreToolUse hook denies an Agent/Task spawn carrying FLOWMAP-CONTRACT:<id> when <id> has no valid+coherent contract; fails open otherwise.
intent.rationale: makes the contract a 100% gate for the execution lane without blocking recon/verify/analysis subagents.
